title: SAVAGE COMMAND BENCHMARKER - Scientific Analysis Report

majorFindings: 83% of sampled commands violate CLAUDE.md complexity rules (score ≥ 5)
Average command is 9.5x more complex than target (47.6 vs 5)
Commands contain more thinking framework than actual functionality
Zero evidence of the '3-Question Rule' being applied in practice
Massive token waste through repetitive thinking boilerplate

violations: Complexity score 17.8x over limit (89 vs 5)
Multiple abstraction layers without justification
16 different thinking blocks create cognitive overload
Recursive meta-thinking about thinking (inception syndrome)
Zero concrete implementation, 100% framework fluff
intelligentCodeEnhancer.savageAssessment: This command has more thinking overhead than a PhD thesis committee. 89 complexity points for what should be a simple 'improve this code' task. That's not intelligence, that's bureaucratic bloat with a PhD in overthinking.

violations: Complexity score 25.4x over limit (127 vs 5)
819 lines for what should be template generation
Multiple redundant checklists and thinking frameworks
Violates its own simplicity principles while preaching them
More meta-commentary than actual generation logic
javaCleanCodeGenerator.savageAssessment: This 819-line behemoth preaches simplicity while being the antithesis of it. It's like a fitness guru who's 400 pounds teaching diet tips. The irony is so thick you could use it as concrete.

violations: Complexity score 15.2x over limit (76 vs 5)
Router to manage complexity that is itself too complex
Recursive complexity assessment of complexity assessment
No actual routing logic, just framework about routing
Meta-meta-thinking about thinking strategies
adaptiveComplexityRouter.savageAssessment: A router designed to handle complexity that is itself a complexity nightmare. It's like hiring a consultant to tell you that you need fewer consultants. 76 points for what should be an if-else statement.

violations: Complexity score 6.8x over limit (34 vs 5)
250 lines of 'rapid' that is anything but rapid
Delegates to other complex commands when complexity > 3
Thinking overhead makes it slower than manual coding
Still violates simplicity while claiming to be 'rapid'
javaRapidImplementation.savageAssessment: 250 lines of 'rapid' implementation that moves at the speed of bureaucracy. It's like a fast-food restaurant with a 45-minute ordering process. The only thing rapid about this is how quickly it violates its own principles.

violations: Complexity score 22.4x over limit (112 vs 5)
673 lines of 'interactive' thinking framework
More abstraction layers than a wedding cake
JavaScript pseudo-code that wouldn't run anywhere
Confidence percentages are completely arbitrary
ultrathinkInteractive.savageAssessment: This 673-line monstrosity claims to be 'interactive' but reading it is about as interactive as watching paint dry in a library. The 'confidence' metrics are as scientifically rigorous as a horoscope.

successRate.note: Success defined as producing usable output within complexity limits

compositionCompatibility.note: Most commands reference other complex commands, creating circular dependencies

thinkingOverhead.description: Every command has massive thinking framework overhead

complexityViolations.description: 80% of commands exceed complexity limits by 5x+

recursiveComplexity.description: Commands about complexity are themselves complex
recursiveComplexity.recommendation: Delete and start over with single-purpose commands

pseudoCodeDisaster.description: JavaScript pseudo-code that doesn't actually work
pseudoCodeDisaster.recommendation: Replace with actual working shell commands

roi.interpretation: Commands are 23.4x more expensive to use than manual execution with 4x lower success rate

immediate: 🔥 DELETE 90% of thinking framework boilerplate immediately
Apply actual 3-Question Rule: Can I use what exists? Can I solve with simple method? Do I need abstraction NOW?
Replace pseudo-JavaScript with actual working shell commands
Enforce complexity limit of 5 with automatic rejection of anything higher
Remove all recursive self-references between commands
strategic: Rewrite commands to be single-purpose functions under 50 lines
Implement actual statistical validation instead of arbitrary confidence scores
Create functional test suite that validates commands actually work
Measure token efficiency and optimize for cost-effectiveness
Focus on doing one thing well instead of framework building

keyFailings: Commands violate their own stated principles with religious fervor
More thinking about thinking than actual thinking
Complexity scores that would make enterprise Java architects blush
Token consumption worthy of a cryptocurrency mining operation
Success rates that would get you fired from a coin-flipping job
savageSummary.finalVerdict: These commands are like hiring a committee to decide on the color of a stop sign, then forming a subcommittee to think about thinking about the committee's thinking process. The 'intelligent' code enhancer couldn't intelligently enhance a hello world program without a 16-step meta-cognitive framework analysis.
savageSummary.recommendedAction: CTRL+A, DELETE, start over with the revolutionary concept of actually doing what the user asked for.

methodology.complexityScoring: Based on CLAUDE.md rules: direct solution (1) + classes (2 each) + interfaces (1 each) + patterns (3 each) + config (2 each)
methodology.biasDisclosure: Author has strong bias toward functional simplicity and against complexity theater
